goal: Add notes rewrite recovery doctor and migration commands

summary.title: Recover Mainline notes after history rewrites
summary.what: Add a notes recovery surface: doctor --notes diagnoses unreachable mainline commit notes and likely rewrite drift; migrate notes previews and applies safe migrations from old note keys to current-main commits using commit-map, tree-hash, and patch-id matching. The migration path defaults to dry-run, writes only safe migrations, merges note payloads instead of overwriting, and pushes only with force-with-lease after local notes match the remote notes ref.
summary.why: A history rewrite can change commit SHA values while refs/notes/mainline/intents still points at old commits. Mainline then stops seeing merge evidence on current main and can show many already-merged intents as proposed. Operators need a first-class diagnosis and controlled recovery path instead of ad hoc scripts.
summary.user_goal: Ship a first version of notes rewrite recovery without adding safety snapshots yet, then dogfood it on the Traveloka history rewrite backup.

decisions.chose: split read-only diagnosis from explicit migration
decisions.rationale: doctor --notes can be run safely during incident triage, while migrate notes makes writes and pushes explicit operator decisions.

decisions.chose: commit-map first, then unique tree-hash and unique patch-id inference
decisions.rationale: commit-map is exact when available; tree and patch matches cover common rebase/rewrite cases but are only auto-written when unique.

decisions.chose: push notes only with force-with-lease and only when local notes equal remote notes before migration
decisions.rationale: notes are a shared ref, so a stale local migration must not overwrite remote notes that were not part of the local plan.

rejected.alternative: hide the repair inside sync or doctor --fix
rejected.reason: sync can auto-pin and push notes today; recovery writes need an explicit command so operators understand and can dry-run the shared-ref change.
wrongly pinning an intent to the wrong commit is worse than leaving the note unresolved for manual review.

risks: Unique tree-hash or patch-id inference can still be a false positive if two independent commits produce identical content or diff; the command therefore keeps non-unique matches in review_required and should be used with dry-run output during incidents.

anti_patterns.what: Do not make notes recovery an implicit side effect of ordinary read commands
anti_patterns.why: refs/notes/mainline/intents is shared team state; incident repair must remain explicit and reviewable.

followups: Add an explicit plan/apply file format for review_required migrations once manual candidate acceptance is needed.
Consider a future safety snapshot command to make rewrite recovery deterministic instead of inferred.
review_notes: Dogfood used the 2026-05-05 history-rewrite backup by combining rewritten main with pre-rewrite notes: doctor detected 238 unreachable mainline notes, commit-map dry-run found 219 safe migrations and 19 unresolved, and local write moved the safe notes so likely_history_rewrite became false.

Focused validation: go test ./internal/engine -run TestMigrateNotesInferMovesUnreachableNoteByTreeHash -count=1; go test ./internal/cli -run TestNonExistent -count=1; go test ./internal/gitops -count=1. A broader go test ./internal/gitops ./internal/engine ./internal/cli run was stopped after the engine package exceeded several minutes.

architectural_claims: notes recovery is explicit migration tooling, not an implicit sync side effect
refs/notes/mainline/intents remains the source of merged intent evidence
safe migrations merge existing note payloads and remove old unreachable note keys
behavioral_changes: mainline doctor --notes reports notes reachability and likely rewrite drift
mainline migrate notes previews commit-map/tree-hash/patch-id note migrations by default
mainline migrate notes --write applies only safe migrations locally
mainline migrate notes --push uses force-with-lease and refuses stale local notes
security_implications: Push path refuses to update remote notes if local notes are not already at the remote notes ref, reducing accidental shared-ref data loss.
migration_notes: Existing repositories do not need a schema migration; this adds recovery commands for incident use.
